join_order.description: Single pass over lineitem (59.9M rows): build hash_map<l_orderkey, sum(l_quantity)>, then threshold-filter to compact_hash_set<qualifying_orderkeys> where sum > 30000 (scaled: 300 * 100)

join_order.note: Load pre-built hash_c_custkey index via mmap; perform ~57 point lookups to fetch c_name. O(mmap) build cost ~0ms.
Load pre-built hash_l_orderkey multi-value index via mmap; for each of ~57 qualifying orderkeys, fetch all matching lineitem positions and accumulate SUM(l_quantity). O(mmap) build cost ~0ms.

subquery_detail.type: IN (SELECT ... GROUP BY ... HAVING SUM > threshold)
subquery_detail.plan: Phase 1: single scan of lineitem l_quantity column, build hash_map<int32_t orderkey, int64_t qty_sum>. After scan, iterate map and insert orderkeys with qty_sum > 30000 into compact_hash_set<int32_t>. Scale factor: l_quantity stored as int64_t with scale_factor=2, so threshold = 300 * 100 = 30000.
subquery_detail.critical_note: SCALE FACTOR: l_quantity has scale_factor=2, so 300 units = 30000 in raw stored values. Use 30000 as the HAVING threshold.

logical_plan.aggregation: hash_group_by (tiny cardinality ~57 groups — effectively flat in-register; use compact_hash_map pre-sized to 64)

filter_pushdown.applied_at: during orders scan via hash_semi_join probe

late_materialization.description: c_name (VARCHAR) is loaded only after orders scan identifies ~57 qualifying custkeys, using hash_c_custkey index for targeted point lookups. Avoids scanning 1.5M customer rows.

data_structures.phase1_qty_map: compact_hash_map<int32_t, int64_t>(pre_sized_power2 >= 15M * 1.33 ≈ 33554432) — open-addressing, robin-hood or linear probing. One entry per unique l_orderkey. Built in parallel with thread-local maps + merge.
data_structures.qualifying_orderkeys_set: compact_hash_set<int32_t>(pre_sized 128) — holds ~57 qualifying orderkeys after HAVING filter. Used for O(1) semi-join probe during orders scan.
data_structures.orders_semi_join_probe: orders full scan probing qualifying_orderkeys_set; matching rows collected into small vector (~57 entries) with columns: o_orderkey, o_custkey, o_orderdate, o_totalprice
data_structures.customer_lookup: prebuilt_index_load(hash_c_custkey) — mmap the 16MB pre-built single-value hash index; ~57 point lookups to resolve c_name. No runtime hash table build.
data_structures.lineitem_lookup: prebuilt_index_load(hash_l_orderkey) — mmap the ~400MB pre-built multi-value hash index; ~57 multi-value lookups, read l_quantity at each returned position, accumulate sum.
data_structures.aggregation: flat_vector<AggEntry>(~57 entries) — struct { c_name, c_custkey, o_orderkey, o_orderdate, o_totalprice, sum_qty }; one entry per qualifying order. No hash table needed at this scale.

phase1_parallel_lineitem_scan.description: Parallel morsel-driven scan of l_orderkey and l_quantity columns (2 columns only). Each thread maintains a thread-local compact_hash_map<int32_t, int64_t> accumulating sum(l_quantity) per orderkey. After parallel phase, merge thread-local maps into a single global map by iterating each local map and adding to global. Then single-pass filter: iterate global map, insert keys with sum > 30000 into qualifying_orderkeys_set.
phase1_parallel_lineitem_scan.note: Only load l_orderkey and l_quantity — skip all other lineitem columns. This halves I/O vs full row scan.

phase2_orders_scan.description: Sequential scan of o_orderkey column. For each row, probe qualifying_orderkeys_set (tiny ~57 entry set, fits in L1 cache). On match, collect o_orderkey, o_custkey, o_orderdate, o_totalprice into result vector. Expected ~57 matches from 15M rows.
phase2_orders_scan.parallelism: parallel scan with 64 threads, thread-local match vectors, merge after. Qualifying set is read-only so no synchronization needed during probe.
phase2_orders_scan.note: qualifying_orderkeys_set is tiny (~57 entries), so it stays hot in L1 cache across all threads.

phase3_customer_lookup.description: For each of ~57 qualifying orders, perform point lookup in pre-built hash_c_custkey index using o_custkey as key. Returns position in c_name column. Load c_name string at that position only. Sequential — ~57 lookups, negligible cost.

phase4_lineitem_quantity_sum.description: For each of ~57 qualifying orderkeys, perform multi-value lookup in pre-built hash_l_orderkey index. For each returned position list, load l_quantity values and accumulate sum. Attach final sum_qty to each result row. Sequential — ~57 lookups.

phase5_sort_topk.description: Sort ~57 result rows by (o_totalprice DESC, o_orderdate ASC). Since result set << LIMIT 100, return all rows sorted. Use std::sort with compound comparator — at 57 elements this is O(57 log 57) ≈ trivial.

parallelism.synchronization: thread_local_merge for phase1 qty_map; read_only_shared for qualifying_orderkeys probe

index_usage.purpose: point_lookup_customer_by_custkey — avoids full customer table scan (1.5M rows). ~57 lookups after qualifying orders identified.
multi_value_lookup_lineitem_by_orderkey — retrieve all lineitem positions for ~57 qualifying orderkeys to compute SUM(l_quantity) in phase 4. Avoids second full lineitem scan.

scan_details.lineitem_phase1: Single columnar scan of l_orderkey + l_quantity only (2 of N columns). Parallel morsel-driven. This is the dominant I/O cost.
scan_details.orders_phase2: Single columnar scan of o_orderkey + o_custkey + o_orderdate + o_totalprice. Parallel morsel-driven. Probe qualifying_orderkeys_set inline.
scan_details.customer: No scan — use hash_c_custkey index for targeted lookups only
scan_details.lineitem_phase4: No full scan — use hash_l_orderkey index for targeted multi-value lookups only

physical_plan.memory_pattern: streaming_sequential for phase1 and phase2 scans; random_access_with_prefetch for index lookups in phase3 and phase4

memory_budget.phase1_thread_local_maps: 64 threads × compact_hash_map(~15M/64 entries avg) ≈ 64 × ~1MB = ~64MB peak during parallel build

phase_breakdown.phase1_lineitem_scan_build_qty_map: ~60-80ms (59.9M rows × 2 cols, parallel 64 threads, HDD I/O bound)
phase_breakdown.phase2_orders_scan_semi_join: ~20-30ms (15M rows × 4 cols, parallel, qualifying_set in L1)
phase_breakdown.index_mmap_load: ~5-10ms (mmap hash_c_custkey + hash_l_orderkey — OS page faults on first access)

estimated_cost.target_comparison: DuckDB 175ms, Umbra 180ms — target <150ms by exploiting pre-built indexes to eliminate second full lineitem scan and full customer scan
